citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
At the time of the inspection, it could not be determined how many visits the Attending Veterinarian (AV) made to the
facility in 2025. The written program of veterinary care states that the attending veterinarian visits are to be done "twice a
year." Several calls were made to query the AV visits, and on Monday, January 26, the AV contacted the inspector and
recalled that he visited once in 2025. A later conversation with his employee confirmed there was only one AV visit to the
premises, which occurred on April 30, 2025.
Veterinary visits are essential to ensure the health and welfare of the animals at the facility. Especially in the case of
nonhuman primates and birds, the AV can observe if the programs of environmental enhancement/enrichment are being
followed as directed. Other subjects, such as animal nutrition, husbandry, and pest control, can be monitored and
addressed as necessary to enhance animal health and welfare at the facility.
Each dealer and exhibitor shall employ an attending veterinarian under formal arrangements. In the case of a part-time
attending veterinarian or consultant arrangements, the formal arrangements shall include a written program of veterinary
care and regularly scheduled visits to the premises of the dealer or exhibitor.
Correct by February 28, 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with zoo staff.
n

narrative:
2.40(a)(1) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
At the time of the inspection, it could not be determined how many visits the Attending Veterinarian (AV) made to the 
facility in 2025. The written program of veterinary care states that the attending veterinarian visits are to be done "twice a 
year." Several calls were made to query the AV visits, and on Monday, January 26, the AV contacted the inspector and 
recalled that he visited once in 2025. A later conversation with his employee confirmed there was only one AV visit to the 
premises, which occurred on April 30, 2025. 
 
Veterinary visits are essential to ensure the health and welfare of the animals at the facility. Especially in the case of 
nonhuman primates and birds, the AV can observe if the programs of environmental enhancement/enrichment are being 
followed as directed. Other subjects, such as animal nutrition, husbandry, and pest control, can be monitored and 
addressed as necessary to enhance animal health and welfare at the facility. 
 
Each dealer and exhibitor shall employ an attending veterinarian under formal arrangements. In the case of a part-time 
attending veterinarian or consultant arrangements, the formal arrangements shall include a written program of veterinary 
care and regularly scheduled visits to the premises of the dealer or exhibitor. 
 
Correct by February 28, 2026. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with zoo staff. 
 
 n
